examples.text: إِنَّ أُحُدًا جَبَلٌ يُحِبُّنَا وَنُحِبُّهُ وَهُوَ عَلَى تُرْعَةٍ مِنْ تُرَعِ الْجَنَّةِ وَعَيْرٌ عَلَى تُرْعَةٍ مِنْ تُرَعِ النَّارِ.
examples.english: ʾuḥud is a mountain which loves us and we love it, and it stands at one of the gates of Paradise. And ʿayr stands at one of the gates of Hell.’”

senses.gloss: mouth of a rivulet, of a watering-trough, of a water-tank or of an other source of water

etymology.text: Borrowed from Aramaic תַּרְעָא (tarʿā, “opening, gate”). Doublet of ثُغْرَة (ṯuḡra).

body.text: a place hollowed out by the water in the side of a river, whence it flows forth:
: (Msb:) in the S it is said to signify the
but correctly the sentence should be,
channel of water, to a meadow

(L, TA:) this is the meaning commonly known [in the present day: the general name in Egypt for a canal cut for the purpose of irrigation, conveying the water of the Nile through the adjacent fields]. (TA.) ―         -b3-  The
by which the water enters, and where the people draw it:
(Az, Mgh, * K, * TA:) and, (K,) accord. to AA, (TA,) the
station of the drinkers at the watering-trough
as in the O and K; or, as in the L, the
which is the station of the drinkers.
(K:) if in low land, it is called
a flight of steps by which one ascends;
: (S, K:) so accord. to some in a trad., which see in what follows: (S, * TA:) and particularly the
flight of steps of a pulpit.
He opened the door of the house.
(TA.) And it is said in a trad.,

body.text_bw: A_in~a minobariy h`*aA EalaY turoEapK mino turaEi
body.text_ar: اـِنَّ مِنْبَرِي هٰذَا عَلَى تُرْعَةٍ مِنْ تُرَعِ

body.text: Verily this my pulpit is at a gate of the gates of Paradise:
thus explained by Sahl Ibn-Saad Es-Sá'idee, the relater of the trad.; and A'Obeyd says,
[“ and it is the proper, ” or “ the valid and obvious, way, ” of explaining it], meaning that it is the preferable explanation: but the author of the K, mistaking his meaning, makes

: or the meaning of this trad. is, he who acts according to the exhortations recited upon the steps of my pulpit will enter Paradise: or, accord. to Kt, prayer and praise in this place are means of attaining to Paradise; so that it is as though it were a portion of Paradise. (TA.) In the same manner Sahl explained his other trad,,

body.text_bw: A_in~a qadamiY EalaY turoEapK mino turaEi AlHawoDi
body.text_ar: اـِنَّ قَدَمِى عَلَى تُرْعَةٍ مِنْ تُرَعِ الحَوْضِ

body.text: Verily my foot is at a gate of the gates of the pool
